description_long: This object consists of three cardboard boxes, each appearing worn and partially crushed, stacked together in a casual arrangement. The boxes are sealed and reinforced with wide strips of silver-gray duct tape, some of which are peeling or loosely attached. The cardboard surfaces are creased, dented, and show signs of heavy use, with flaps bent outward and edges frayed. The boxes vary slightly in size but are all medium to large, typical for shipping or moving parcels. The color is a muted brown, characteristic of standard corrugated cardboard, with the tape providing a contrasting metallic sheen. The overall appearance suggests these parcels have been handled roughly, possibly discarded or awaiting collection in an urban alley or sidewalk.
description: Three battered cardboard boxes, taped with silver-gray duct tape, stacked together with bent flaps and worn surfaces.
description_view_0: Front view showing two boxes at the base and one on top, with open flaps and visible tape seams.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the vertical stacking and the prominent tape strips on the box edges.
description_view_2: Angled view displaying the overlapping arrangement and the most visible tape patches on the box tops.
description_view_3: Top-down view revealing the open flaps and the irregular, dented surfaces of all three boxes.
